source_paper: Adamson & Smith 2018 NEJM doi.org/10.1056/NEJMp1714529 — documented higher miss rate for FST IV-VI due to amelanotic presentation and lower index of suspicion on dark skin
Kaufman et al. 2018 JAAD — erythema less visible on dark skin; papular and follicular variants more common but under-recognized in AI models
Alexis et al. 2016 J Drugs Dermatol — plaques appear violaceous/hyperpigmented rather than salmon-pink on FST IV-VI; scale less apparent
Davis & Callender 2010 J Clin Aesthet Dermatol — post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation dominates presentation; inflammatory lesion grading trained on FST I-III underperforms
Alexis 2010 J Drugs Dermatol — petaloid and pityriasiform variants predominate on dark skin; AI trained on erythematous patches fails to generalize
Schwartz 2004 Int J Dermatol — hypopigmented macules on FST V-VI frequently misclassified as vitiligo or pityriasis alba; hyperpigmented variant on FST I-III better represented in training data
